@@ -467,7 +467,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-kj5","title":"config Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br config` command for viewing and modifying br configuration. Supports the layered configuration system (CLI > env > project > user > SQLite > defaults).

## CLI Interface
```
br config [SUBCOMMAND] [OPTIONS]

Subcommands:
  get <key>                 Get a config value
  set <key> <value>         Set a config value
  unset <key>               Remove a config value
  list                      List all config values
  edit                      Open config in editor
  path                      Show config file paths
  init                      Create default config file

Options:
  --global                  Use user-level config (~/.config/br/config.yaml)
  --project                 Use project-level config (.beads/config.yaml)
  --db                      Use SQLite-stored config
  --show-source             Show where each value comes from
  --json                    Output as JSON
```

## Technical Requirements

### Configuration Layering
Priority order (highest to lowest):
1. CLI flags (--prefix, etc.)
2. Environment variables (BR_PREFIX, etc.)
3. Project config (.beads/config.yaml)
4. User config (~/.config/br/config.yaml)
5. SQLite config table
6. Hard-coded defaults

```rust
pub struct ConfigResolver {
    cli_args: HashMap<String, String>,
    env_prefix: &str,
    project_config: Option<Config>,
    user_config: Option<Config>,
    db_config: HashMap<String, String>,
    defaults: HashMap<String, String>,
}

impl ConfigResolver {
    pub fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<ConfigValue> {
        // Check each layer in order
        if let Some(v) = self.cli_args.get(key) {
            return Some(ConfigValue { value: v.clone(), source: ConfigSource::Cli });
        }
        if let Some(v) = std::env::var(format!("BR_{}", key.to_uppercase())).ok() {
            return Some(ConfigValue { value: v, source: ConfigSource::Env });
        }
        // ... continue through layers
    }
    
    pub fn get_all_with_sources(&self) -> Vec<(String, ConfigValue)> {
        // Return all config keys with their effective values and sources
    }
}
```

### Config File Format (YAML)
```yaml
# .beads/config.yaml
prefix: bd                    # Issue ID prefix
default_priority: 2           # Default priority for new issues
default_type: task            # Default issue type
auto_flush: true              # Auto-export after changes
sync_on_init: true            # Import JSONL on init if newer
editor: ${EDITOR:-vim}        # Editor for edit commands

# Output preferences
color: auto                   # auto, always, never
format: human                 # human, json, robot

# Paths
database: .beads/bd.db        # SQLite database path
jsonl_dir: .beads             # JSONL export directory
```

### YAML-Only vs SQLite Keys
From the documentation:
- **YAML-only**: editor, color, format, paths (never stored in SQLite)
- **SQLite-capable**: prefix, default_priority, default_type, auto_flush

### Environment Variables
```
BR_PREFIX          - Issue ID prefix
BR_DEFAULT_PRIORITY - Default priority
BR_COLOR           - Color output (auto/always/never)
BR_FORMAT          - Output format
BR_DATABASE        - Database path
```

## Output Formats

### config list (human)
```
Configuration (merged from all sources):

Key               Value          Source
───────────────────────────────────────────
prefix            bd             project (.beads/config.yaml)
default_priority  2              default
default_type      task           default
auto_flush        true           user (~/.config/br/config.yaml)
color             auto           env (BR_COLOR)
editor            vim            default
```

### config list --json
```json
{
  "values": {
    "prefix": { "value": "bd", "source": "project" },
    "default_priority": { "value": "2", "source": "default" }
  },
  "paths": {
    "project": ".beads/config.yaml",
    "user": "/home/user/.config/br/config.yaml"
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] config get <key> returns effective value
- [ ] config set <key> <value> writes to appropriate config
- [ ] config unset <key> removes config value
- [ ] config list shows all values with sources
- [ ] --global flag targets user config
- [ ] --project flag targets project config
- [ ] --db flag targets SQLite config
- [ ] config path shows all config file locations
- [ ] config edit opens editor
- [ ] Environment variable override works
- [ ] Layering priority is correct

## Unit Tests
- Default values returned when no config
- Project config overrides default
- User config falls back when no project config
- Env vars override file config
- CLI args override everything
- YAML-only keys never read from SQLite
- set/get round-trip works
- unset removes value

## Dependencies
- SQLite Storage Layer (for db config)
- Model Types (Config struct)

## Rationale
Layered configuration allows users to set project-specific preferences while maintaining personal defaults. The config command makes this system transparent and debuggable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:34.672384320Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:49:40.702920628Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:49:40.702920628Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of beads_rust-tqs (config command aligned to updated config system)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-kptp","title":"Integrate rich output into lint command","description":"## Command: br lint [--fix]

### Traffic Level: LOW-MEDIUM
Data quality checks with optional auto-fix.

### Current Implementation
Location: src/cli/commands/lint.rs
Output: List of warnings/errors

### Integration Steps
1. Show progress for large issue sets
2. Group warnings by type
3. Highlight fixable vs manual issues
4. Show fix preview with --dry-run

### Visual Enhancement
```
╭─ Lint Results ──────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  ✓ 156 issues checked                               │
│                                                     │
│  ⚠ 3 warnings:                                      │
│  ├── beads_rust-abc1  Missing description           │
│  ├── beads_rust-def2  Orphaned dependency           │
│  └── beads_rust-ghi3  Invalid priority (fixed)      │
│                                                     │
│  ❌ 1 error:                                         │
│  └── beads_rust-jkl4  Circular dependency detected  │
│                                                     │
│  Auto-fixable: 1 issue                              │
│  Run with --fix to apply fixes                      │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Location: tests/cli/lint_tests.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_lint_groups_by_type() {
    let warnings = create_lint_warnings();
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_lint_results(&warnings, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("warning") || output.contains("⚠"));
}

#[test]
fn test_lint_json_unchanged() {
    let current = run_lint_json();
    let baseline = load_fixture("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/lint.json");
    assert_json_eq!(current, baseline);
}

#[test]
fn test_lint_shows_fixable() {
    let warnings = vec![LintWarning { fixable: true, .. }];
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_lint_results(&warnings, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("fixable") || output.contains("--fix"));
}
```

### E2E Test Script
Location: tests/e2e/lint_e2e.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Lint Command ==="
setup_test_db
create_issues_with_lint_problems

log_step "Testing basic lint"
OUTPUT=$(br lint 2>&1)
assert_contains "$OUTPUT" "checked" "Should show checked count"
log_pass "Basic lint works"

log_step "Testing --fix dry run"
FIX_OUTPUT=$(br lint --fix --dry-run 2>&1)
log_pass "--fix dry run works"

log_step "Testing JSON output"
JSON_OUTPUT=$(br lint --json)
echo "$JSON_OUTPUT" | jq . > /dev/null
log_pass "JSON output valid"

log_success "=== Lint command E2E: ALL PASSED ==="
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log issue count being checked
- Log warning/error counts by type
- Log fixes applied (with --fix)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:35:39.832516428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:37:41.737560347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:37:41.737509110Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-kptp","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-1rpr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-kptp","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-31nl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-kqok","title":"Track: dep list should support sorting by priority instead of only by ID","description":"SUBTASK 7.3 (DEFERRED FEATURE): src/cli/commands/dep.rs:903 contains:
  "For now, let us sort by ID to be deterministic, or fetch details. Priority sorting would require fetching issues."

CURRENT BEHAVIOR: The dep list command sorts dependencies by ID string (lexicographic). This is deterministic but not useful for triage — a user running br dep list bd-X wants to see the highest-priority blockers first.

DESIRED BEHAVIOR: Add a --sort=priority flag (or make priority the default) that fetches the full issue for each dependency and sorts by priority descending.

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS:
- The current code avoids fetching issues for performance (dep list only queries the dependencies table)
- Priority sorting requires N additional get_issue() calls (one per dependency) which adds latency for issues with many deps
- Could batch-fetch with a single SQL query: SELECT * FROM issues WHERE id IN (dep1, dep2, ...) ORDER BY priority ASC
- For most issues, N < 10 deps, so the performance impact is negligible
- Could cache the results since the same issue might appear in multiple dep lists
- Consider making ID sort the default for --json (deterministic, machine-friendly) and priority sort the default for interactive/rich mode (human-friendly)

PRIORITY: P4/backlog because the current behavior is correct (deterministic, complete), just not optimally sorted for human use.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:41.314015226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:58:59.092024775Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:58:59.091676463Z","close_reason":"Made human dep list output priority-aware while preserving JSON/TOON machine output order","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-kr5i","title":"E2E tests: label command","description":"# E2E Tests for `label` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br label add <issue_id> <label>` - Add label to issue
- `br label remove <issue_id> <label>` - Remove label from issue
- `br label list` - List all labels in workspace
- `br label --json` - JSON output mode

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Add single label, verify via show
2. Add multiple labels to same issue
3. Remove label, verify removed
4. List all labels across issues
5. Add same label to multiple issues

### Error Cases
6. Add label to non-existent issue → error
7. Remove non-existent label → error or no-op
8. Invalid label format (if any validation)

### Edge Cases
9. Label with special characters
10. Very long label name
11. Case sensitivity (bug vs BUG)
12. Label on closed issue

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_labels.rs
- [ ] 12+ test functions
- [ ] Verify label persistence in JSONL export","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenSparrow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:25:51.989260181Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T14:44:10.369726951Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T14:44:10.369726951Z","close_reason":"Implemented 16 E2E tests for label command in tests/e2e_labels.rs - all tests passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-kr5i","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 ## 5) Sync (end of session)
@@ -47,7 +47,7 @@ br sync --flush-only
 - Preferred flags:
   - Use `--format json` or `--format toon` when the command supports it.
   - `--json` always forces JSON.
-  - Some commands also accept `--robot` (alias for `--json`).
+  - For mutation commands such as `update` and `close`, prefer global `--json`; do not assume every mutation command has command-local `--format`.
 - When scripting, route stderr separately; errors may be emitted as structured JSON on stderr.
 
 ## Agent smoke test

@@ -21,6 +21,7 @@ need_cmd() {
 
 need_cmd jq
 need_cmd tru
+need_cmd grep
 
 ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
 
@@ -53,21 +54,35 @@ log "Create 3 issues"
 "$BR" create "Smoke two" --type bug --priority 0 --json >/dev/null
 "$BR" create "Smoke three" --type feature --priority 1 --json >/dev/null
 
-ID1=$("$BR" list --format json --limit 1 | jq -r ".[0].id")
+list_is_array_or_page='if type=="array" then true else (has("issues") and (.issues | type=="array")) end'
+first_issue_id='if type=="array" then .[0].id else .issues[0].id end'
+
+ID1=$("$BR" list --format json --limit 1 | jq -r "$first_issue_id")
 
 log "JSON: list/show parse"
-"$BR" list --format json --limit 5 | jq -e "type==\"array\"" >/dev/null
+"$BR" list --format json --limit 5 | jq -e "$list_is_array_or_page" >/dev/null
 "$BR" show "$ID1" --format json | jq -e 'if type=="array" then (.[0] | has("id") and has("title")) else (has("id") and has("title")) end' >/dev/null
 
+log "Docs: agent quickstarts do not put --format on mutation commands"
+if grep -En 'br (update|close|create|delete|reopen|defer|undefer|label add|label remove)\b.*--format' \
+    "$ROOT/docs/agent/QUICKSTART.md" "$ROOT/docs/agent/EXAMPLES.md"; then
+    log "Unsupported --format flag found on mutation-command examples"
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+log "JSON: mutation commands parse via global --json"
+"$BR" --json update "$ID1" --status in_progress --claim | jq -e 'if type=="array" then (.[0].status == "in_progress") else (.status == "in_progress") end' >/dev/null
+"$BR" --json close "$ID1" --reason "agent smoke test" | jq -e 'if type=="array" then (.[0].status == "closed") else (.status == "closed") end' >/dev/null
+
 log "TOON: list/show decode+parse"
-"$BR" list --format toon --limit 5 | tru --decode | jq -e "type==\"array\"" >/dev/null
+"$BR" list --format toon --limit 5 | tru --decode | jq -e "$list_is_array_or_page" >/dev/null
 "$BR" show "$ID1" --format toon | tru --decode | jq -e 'if type=="array" then (.[0] | has("id") and has("title")) else (has("id") and has("title")) end' >/dev/null
 
 log "Env: TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT defaults output when --format not provided"
-TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT=toon "$BR" list --limit 1 | tru --decode | jq -e "type==\"array\"" >/dev/null
+TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT=toon "$BR" list --limit 1 | tru --decode | jq -e "$list_is_array_or_page" >/dev/null
 
 log "Env: BR_OUTPUT_FORMAT takes precedence over TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT"
-BR_OUTPUT_FORMAT=json TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT=toon "$BR" list --limit 1 | jq -e "type==\"array\"" >/dev/null
+BR_OUTPUT_FORMAT=json TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT=toon "$BR" list --limit 1 | jq -e "$list_is_array_or_page" >/dev/null
 
 log "Error envelope (stderr JSON)"
 ERR_JSON="$WORKDIR/err.json"
